Welcome to Mafia Game 225: Flavorless Mafia

If you are new to AoPS Mafia, please read all of the rules. Even if you are familiar with forum mafia, there may be rules here that you are not familiar with.

Table of Contents
If you have played before, you may read the game-specific rules at the end of this section and skim the other sections. If you are new to AoPS Mafia, please read all of the rules. Even if you are familiar with forum mafia, there may be rules here that you are not familiar with.
  • General: Standard rules for mafia
  • Votes: How voting and hangs work
  • Activity: Procedures for deadlines and activity expectations
  • Etiquette: Rules for general behavior
  • Setup: What roles are possible in this game and how they are determined

Game-Specific Rules:
  • Cycles are 14/2.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Deadline hang is on.
  • Majority hang is on starting D2.
  • Votes are locked at LyLo.
  • Doctors may self-target or consecutively target.
  • Intentional fakevoting is not allowed.
  • The confirm rule is in your role PM.

  • General
    Credit to all the usual suspects.
    • 1.1: Do not post at night.
    • 1.2: Do not quote or pretend to quote private communication with the moderator. Paraphrase, folks.
    • 1.3: Do not discuss the events of this game anywhere you are not explicitly given permission to do so. This includes on and off of AoPS.
    • 1.4: In the same spirit, do not discuss the events of other ongoing games anywhere you are not explicitly given permission to do so. This is a forum-wide rule.
    • 1.5: Do not post anything with the effect of concealing text. This includes white or light grey text and exceedingly small font.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete any posts in this thread unless explicitly given permission to do so. Use EBWOP (Edit By Way Of Post) or EBWON (Edit By Way Of Ninja) if you need to.
    • 1.7: Do not post if you are not a living player in this game.
  • Votes
    Credit again to all the usual suspects.
    • 2.1: Vote in the format “Vote: <player>”. For example, if you want to vote for Telemundo, post “Vote: Telemundo”.
    • 2.2: You may also vote to hang nobody by posting “Vote: No Hang”.
    • 2.3: You may remove your vote by posting “Unvote”. You do not need to do this every time you want to switch votes, but it is appreciated for vote counting purposes.
    • 2.4: Though correctly formatted votes (correct spelling, correct capitalization, etc.) are preferred, improperly formatted votes may be counted if the intent is clear. Do not test this. If your votes are improperly formatted regularly, I will tell you to fix them, and may warn you if it becomes excessive.
    • 2.5: Votes in quote or hide tags will not be counted.
    • 2.6: Do not fakevote.
    • 2.7: Deadline hang is defined as a player being hanged at the day phase’s deadline if the day isn’t ended early otherwise. The player who is hanged will be the player with the most votes. In the case of a tie, the tie is broken randomly, unless the No Hang option is tied; No Hang always wins ties. Deadline Hang will be on for the entire game.
    • 2.8: Majority Hang is defined as a player being hanged once they receive votes from more than half of all players, or nobody being hanged once No Hang receives votes from at least half of all players. Majority Hang will be on starting on the second day phase.
    • 2.9: LyLo (Hang or Lose) is when the number of remaining mafia is one less than the number of remaining townies. In LyLo, if a mafia member is not hanged, town will lose. At LyLo, votes will be locked; once you place a vote, you will be unable to retract or move it.
    • 2.10: Once a player is hanged, or No Hang is determined, the day will end.
    • 2.11: Once I post the end-of-day post, the hanged player, if any, will die, and night will begin.
  • Activity
    Usual suspects copypasta.
    • 3.1: The deadline for a day will be 336 hours (2 weeks) after it starts. This may be extended at my discretion.
    • 3.2: The deadline for a night will be 48 hours after it starts.
    • 3.3: During a day phase, if you do not post for a period of 48 hours, you will receive an In-Game Prod.
    • 3.4: During a day phase, if you do not post for a period of 72 hours, you will receive a PM Prod.
    • 3.5: During a day phase, if you do not post for a period of 120 hours, you will be replaced.
    • 3.6: If I have to prod you too much, I might replace you.
    • 3.7: You may request replacement in the thread in bold or in your role PM. In general, if there is something in the thread you want me to read, post it in bold.
    • 3.8: You may declare V/LA (Vacant/Low Activity) for some period of time in the thread in bold or in your role PM. If the V/LA is excessively long, though, it is preferred that you request replacement.
  • Etiquette
    Usual suspects.
    • 4.1: I (aroodman) am your moderator for this game.
    • 4.2: I will use the colors red (#FF0000), green (#008000) and blue (#0000FF). Do not use these colors or anything similar.
    • 4.3: I may change the rules at any time, and my say in interpreting the rules is final.
    • 4.4: Follow AoPS conduct guidelines.
    • 4.5: Be mature.
    • 4.6: Do not make personal attacks.
    • 4.7: Do not gamethrow.
    • 4.8: Do not angleshoot. Angleshooting is defined as trying to gain information from things other than what is said by players and significant events. For example, do not attempt to determine a player’s alignment from the fact that they replaced out.
    • 4.9: Rules 4.4 through 4.8, as well as rules 1.2 through 1.7, additionally apply to any private venues of communication I may provide.
    • 4.10: Violation of a rule will result in a warning. A player may receive up to 3 warnings. The fourth will result in force-replacement.
  • Setup
    This section is plagiarized verbatim from fdas, full credit to him.

    This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

    In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
    1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
    2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
    3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
    4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
    5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
    6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

    Role PMs
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Vanilla Townie.

    You have no special abilities. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Town Jailkeeper.

    Each night, you may choose a player to jail. That player may not be killed that night, and also may not perform any actions. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Town Cop.

    Each night you may investigate a player to learn their alignment (town/mafia). You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Town Doctor.

    Each night, you may choose a player to protect. That player cannot be killed for that night. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie.

    The first time someone attempts to kill you at night, you will survive. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Town Tracker.

    Each night you may choose a player to track, to learn who, if anyone, your target visited that night. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Mafia Goon. Your teammate is <player>, a <role>. You may talk with your teammate here.

    Each night, you and your teammate may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. You win when the town is eliminated, or nothing can prevent this.

    Oh, and in case you didn’t already realize, the town’s trying to kill you, so uh... yeah, try to stay hidden. Wouldn’t have to say this if people always realized this.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.
    aroodman wrote:
    You are a Mafia Roleblocker. Your teammate is <player>, a Mafia Goon. You may talk with your teammate here.

    Each night, you and your teammate may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. Additionally, each night, you may choose a player to roleblock. That player may not perform any actions that night. You win when the town is eliminated, or nothing can prevent this.

    Oh, and in case you didn’t already realize, the town’s trying to kill you, so uh... yeah, try to stay hidden. Wouldn’t have to say this if people always realized this.

    To confirm, reply to this PM describing your role and win condition.

post_rendered:
The citizens of the Mafia! Forum arose on a day like any other. Nothing looked different. The front doors of each house opened to reveal the same coffee-infused morning folk that would be seen on days before. All the hallmarks of a normal morning made it hard to place what exactly felt different.

Of course, given the prevalence of death in a game of Mafia, the inhabitants knew that drastic change would inevitably strike the town, and the population would soon drastically decrease, but that had yet to begin. Neighbors would soon distrust each other, but the roles hadn’t been distributed yet. Everyone felt something off that could not be easily attributed to the upcoming bloodbath.

They passed looks of understanding between each other. There was something unsettling in the air that they couldn’t put into words. Finally, someone found the words.

"Wait... guys..."
"What?"
"Don’t you realize where we are?"

The same roads. The same homes. Their entire town looked like it was designed by IKEA. The townsfolk saw nothing noteworthy in their surroundings.

"No, no, don’t you get it? Everything around us!"
"Still don’t see it..."
"The houses! The roads! The bushes! All of this shouldn’t exist!"
"I don’t--wait..."
"Hold on..."
"This is flavor! In Flavorless Mafia!"

The news was not received well.

"You’re telling me I have to read this tryhard mod’s thirty paragraphs of creative writing exercises when I signed up for this specific game specifically so I wouldn’t have to?!"
"You’re kidding me..."
"Where is this guy?"
"Everyone! Over here! I found him!"
"You’re kidding me..."
"He’s locked his place up! The coward!"
"Smash his door! Get’im out here!"
"He’s still writing! I can see him typing!"
"You’re kidding me…"
"The game is literally called Flavorless Mafia!"
"You’re gonna wish you never agreed to mod."
"You’re kidding me!"

aroodman was slain by an angry mob on Day 0. He was the Moderator.



...but the flavor didn’t stop.
Who could be writing it this time?

Day 1 has begun and ends on March 3rd at 7:30 PM EST. EST is bEST, get used to me using it throughout this game.

Confirmed:
Everyone

post_rendered:
Ok last copy-pasted resource
I see no reason to type something cool up when there's already things typed up that i can just copy c:

Preliminary things:
Read all of the rules and confirm.
Answer the RQS.
Ask questions if you are confused.
Things to know/do after that:
Your first and foremost duty as town is to find scum.
I guess my first advice, though is to be active and post -- but that doesn't mean one should just post a bunch of fluff (this word means what it sounds like) that does absolutely nothing and clogs up the thread. If you're ever confused or don't know what's happening, ask questions. Ask questions. Ask people why they think this or that. How they reached a conclusion. What evidence do they have. Why don't they interpret a post this way instead of that way. etc. And do something with those answers. Process them and consider their merit and how much of it you'll believe/disbelieve/put in your back pocket.
Note that scum's goal is to not be hanged/suspected. Think about this, draw inferences from it, and keep it in mind as the game proceeds. (Ask me if you have no idea what I'm saying here.)
The way I usually find scum is by noting significant posts that might be AI (alignment indicative -- that tell you whether a person is scum or town). I insert my thoughts on it and move on. If I'm not sure, I ask questions/just leave it there for future reference.
Things that scum tend to do: bandwagon (follow the crowd), lurk (not show up), pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town), hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting), bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks), buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
Terms to know: w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.); v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.); w/v (self explanatory); AI (alignment indicative); NAI (not alignment indicative); reads (thoughts on who is scum or town); RQS (random question[ing?] stage: What mathjay posted in his first post to get to know the players.); RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.); meta (how one has played in previous mafia games); wifom (wine in front of me).

post_rendered:
Because snoozing's plagiarized resource is quite difficult to read, let me revise it:

Goal: find scum
  1. Be active and post your thoughts (but do not fluff and clog up the thread).
  2. If you're confused, ask questions. Even if you're not confused, ask questions. Question why people did this or that. How did they reach a conclusion. Question people you suspect. Why did they did not do something and so on.
  3. And then consider the answers. Would scum or town be more likely to do something? Would scum or town be more likely to answer this way. Would scum want to do something more than town? Would town want to do something more than scum? And why.
  4. Try to be able to explain why you think this way and explain your reads.
terms
General:
  • RQS - Random questioning stage.
  • Reads - thoughts on who is scum and who is town.
  • meta - how one has played in mafia games.
  • wifom - wine in front of me
  • AI - Alignment indicative. Means that the said thing tells you whether the person is scum or town.
  • NAI - Not alignment indicative.
  • v/v - villager/villager. Means that both players mentioned are town.
  • w/w - wolf/wolf. Means that both players mentioned are scum [together].
  • w/v or v/w - One scum, one wolf.
Textbook scumtells:
  • bandwagon - to follow the crowd.
  • buddying - when a scum townreads another scum, perhaps without a good reason, to try to save their partner or make them tr'ed.
  • bussing - when a scum attacks another scum to make it look like they cannot be partners.
  • hedge - to have no definitive stance, to fencesit.
  • lurk - to fail to show up deliberately.
  • pocketing - scum sweet talking or otherwise coaxing a town into townreading them.

Hmm. One other thing that is always missed for some reason:
Do not claim if you are town, whether you are a PR (power role - you have a special power as town) or a VT (vanilla townie... normal person with only a vote). I repeat. Do not claim whether you are a PR or a VT if you are town unless you're about to die, which won't happen for another 12 days.
The rationale behind this is so that you don't help scum narrow down who is or isn't a PR so that they can kill or block those who are a PR. So don't specify whether you are or are not a PR/VT.

NDMath wrote:
Power Roles
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get killed the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia killed is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we hang tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.

MinecraftFox (7, HANGED): NDMath, b12b3b, rosecasket, GammaZero, mathical8, candythecorgi, snoozingnewt
snoozingnewt (1): MinecraftFox

The outrage was soon replaced by a revelation. The flavor writing itself was nothing unusual, despite the unorthodox premise. What was unusual was that the players could read the flavor from within it.

This could be an extreme breach of game integrity, one thought.

"Did I just... hear your thoughts?"
"I think I heard his thoughts too."
Yeah, me too.
"This is an extreme breach of game integrity!"

They began organizing. Each player would think, and each player's thoughts would be broadcast. There was no hiding now. They could reach deeper into the minds of players than they ever would just reading what others said out loud.

Then one of the players said "I am a mafia goon" out loud and they scrapped the whole plan.

MinecraftFox was hanged. She was a Mafia Goon.

Day 1 has ended. Night 1 has begun and ends on March 5 at 8 PM EST. Deadline for submitting night actions is March 5 at 7:30 PM EST.

Process of Elimination

post_rendered:
Hannah requested general scumhunting feedback for her specifically independent of this game, but when I was writing it it basically just turned into feedback for this game, since this is her only town game in months, so I’m putting it here. Should explain the length difference.

NDMath

HannahW


For the most part this one went really well for you.

Your read that I want to focus on the most isn’t actually mathical. I think the conclusion you reached on mathical there was a completely fair one to reach, even if I do take issue with some of the points that got you there. Mainly, the fact that flip-flopping is NAI. Flip-flopping on a flipped wolf has more merit for scrutiny, but less so in the case of you calling mathical out for flip flopping on b1. If you can’t assign significant, plausible scum motivation, and/or you can’t show why the progression is also hard for a townie to have, then the progression is most likely NAI.

I hate progression cases with a passion, and I think this is a pretty good example of why. I get the instinct for why they’re popular; scumhunting is often intangible and progressions aren’t. It can feel like a concrete step you can take to get involved in the discussion. Unfortunately they’re just not very accurate unless you put in the extra hour.

This feedback isn’t about mathical, though. I want to focus more on snoozing, because I think there’s pretty direct room for improvement here. I often dislike using mindmeld, because it can be an easy way to get pocketed, but this was the kind of game where mindmeld was not only necessary but instantly game-winning.

The game started with a pretty dead thread. All three experienced players were futilely encouraging activity. Any of you could’ve slanked out at any time.

Then the big b1 Gamma rose argument broke out, and you all helped in some way to find it as v/v/v. Not only were you all rescuing 2-3 potential LHFs, but you were all closing the PoE on... yourselves. There were countless opportunities to call b1 or Gamma out for overreaction, or to call rose out for self-awareness. You all saw the towny macro past the scummy micro.

There are plenty of cases where mindmeld reads are bad, but it’s usually good to use mindmeld reads with respect to the actual direction that players are pushing the game in, instead of off-the-cuff micros, and especially with regards to reads you're confident in. You were right for the right reasons on b1, Gamma and rose, and those reads were only solidified as the day went on. Given that you knew they were likely all town, ND and snoozing were both playing in a way that you knew was pro-town, in contexts where they didn’t have to at all.

With players competent as both town and scum, the standard effort, content and tone tells will most likely fail. Good wolves know how to bypass all the common filters. Eventually, you will need to look at which team a player is playing for. If they’re helping your team, and they’re helping your team in heaps, and they wouldn’t have to help your team if they weren’t on your team, by Jove they might be on your team.

That was really the only big "oh man she should’ve reached this other conclusion" moment I had reading your play, and your snoozing scumread didn’t actually impact the game at all. Good job.

snoozingnewt


One thing though. If there’s one scum left, don’t go for the hero check on the consensus scum. The consensus scum is screwed anyway; if you aim for the rim of PoE instead, you could get a clear that ends up mattering in the world where the consensus scum is town. Or you could catch an unexpected wolf, that’s nice too.

rosecasket
Good stuff overall.

One important thing to keep in mind is that you’re arguing over who’s scum, not who’s right. It seemed like a lot of the arguments you got into with b1 in particular didn’t have a ton to do with alignment.

candythecorgi
Like I said, you didn’t even mess up. You just got steamrolled by an overpowered town.

There was one line from early on that I thought might have earned a scumread in another playerlist, though:
candythecorgi wrote:
that was just a gut feeling

Early unnecessary passive-aggressiveness can look like a scummy overreaction.

b12b3b
You obvtowned. Hard. That’s always a good thing.

Try not to get into fights that you really don’t need to get into. You, rose and Gamma all ended up wasting a lot of breath over things that turned out to be petty and irrelevant.

This town was able to figure out that you were town, but others might not as easily. They might see you as overreacting and self-aware. It worked here, though. Props for that.

GammaZero
See b12b3b.

In particular, a lot of your early accusations seemed to come from a sense of obligation to be suspicious more than actual suspicion. Not only does this not help find scum because it's less likely to be accurate, but it can make you look suspicious yourself.

MinecraftFox
I can’t say anything new about that one play.

As for your general play, this might also sound like a dead horse beating, but be active. If you aren’t active, there’s nothing else to comment on. If you can’t be active because of IRL stuff, that’s unfortunate and there’s nothing anyone can really do about that. Would recommend declaring V/LA, though.

mathical8
It’s never fun being a miss. It’s never fun being the only miss. Glad you still enjoyed the game, though.

Your inactivity happened for reasons out of your control, and then you got tunneled for reasons out of your control. Town was wrong for the right reasons, though; they zeroed in on candy immediately after your death.

I thought you did a good job of sounding really pure D2. At that point, Hannah was piling on accusations faster than you knew how to deal with, so succumbing to the pressure there is fair. Only advice would be generic stuff like be active, interact, communicate, etc. Eventually you’ll find a way to be towny if you keep doing that, and others will find ways to read you.
post_canonical:
Hannah requested general scumhunting feedback for her specifically independent of this game, but when I was writing it it basically just turned into feedback for this game, since this is her only town game in months, so I’m putting it here. Should explain the length difference.

[hide=NDMath]:omighty:[/hide]
[hide=HannahW]:omighty:

For the most part this one went really well for you.

Your read that I want to focus on the most isn’t actually mathical. I think the conclusion you  reached on mathical there was a completely fair one to reach, even if I do take issue with some of the points that got you there. Mainly, the fact that flip-flopping is NAI. Flip-flopping on a flipped wolf has more merit for scrutiny, but less so in the case of you calling mathical out for flip flopping on b1. If you can’t assign significant, plausible scum motivation, and/or you can’t show why the progression is also hard for a townie to have, then the progression is most likely NAI.

I hate progression cases with a passion, and I think this is a pretty good example of why. I get the instinct for why they’re popular; scumhunting is often intangible and progressions aren’t. It can feel like a concrete step you can take to get involved in the discussion. Unfortunately they’re just not very accurate unless you put in the extra hour.

This feedback isn’t about mathical, though. I want to focus more on snoozing, because I think there’s pretty direct room for improvement here. I often dislike using mindmeld, because it can be an easy way to get pocketed, but this was the kind of game where mindmeld was not only necessary but instantly game-winning.

The game started with a pretty dead thread. All three experienced players were futilely encouraging activity. Any of you could’ve slanked out at any time.

Then the big b1 Gamma rose argument broke out, and you all helped in some way to find it as v/v/v. Not only were you all rescuing 2-3 potential LHFs, but you were all closing the PoE on... yourselves. There were countless opportunities to call b1 or Gamma out for overreaction, or to call rose out for self-awareness. You all saw the towny macro past the scummy micro.

There are plenty of cases where mindmeld reads are bad, but it’s usually good to use mindmeld reads with respect to the actual direction that players are pushing the game in, instead of off-the-cuff micros, and especially with regards to reads you're confident in. You were right for the right reasons on b1, Gamma and rose, and those reads were only solidified as the day went on. Given that you knew they were likely all town, ND and snoozing were both playing in a way that you knew was pro-town, in contexts where they didn’t have to at all.

With players competent as both town and scum, the standard effort, content and tone tells will most likely fail. Good wolves know how to bypass all the common filters. Eventually, you will need to look at which team a player is playing for. If they’re helping your team, and they’re helping your team in heaps, and they wouldn’t have to help your team if they weren’t on your team, by Jove they might be on your team.

That was really the only big "oh man she should’ve reached this other conclusion" moment I had reading your play, and your snoozing scumread didn’t actually impact the game at all. Good job.[/hide]
[hide=snoozingnewt]:omighty:

One thing though. If there’s one scum left, don’t go for the hero check on the consensus scum. The consensus scum is screwed anyway; if you aim for the rim of PoE instead, you could get a clear that ends up mattering in the world where the consensus scum is town. Or you could catch an unexpected wolf, that’s nice too.[/hide]
[hide=rosecasket]Good stuff overall.

One important thing to keep in mind is that you’re arguing over who’s scum, not who’s right. It seemed like a lot of the arguments you got into with b1 in particular didn’t have a ton to do with alignment.[/hide]
[hide=candythecorgi]Like I said, you didn’t even mess up. You just got steamrolled by an overpowered town.

There was one line from early on that I thought might have earned a scumread in another playerlist, though:

[quote=candythecorgi]that was just a gut feeling :|[/quote]

Early unnecessary passive-aggressiveness can look like a scummy overreaction.[/hide]
[hide=b12b3b]You obvtowned. Hard. That’s always a good thing.

Try not to get into fights that you really don’t need to get into. You, rose and Gamma all ended up wasting a lot of breath over things that turned out to be petty and irrelevant.

This town was able to figure out that you were town, but others might not as easily. They might see you as overreacting and self-aware. It worked here, though. Props for that.[/hide]
[hide=GammaZero]See b12b3b.

In particular, a lot of your early accusations seemed to come from a sense of obligation to be suspicious more than actual suspicion. Not only does this not help find scum because it's less likely to be accurate, but it can make you look suspicious yourself.[/hide]
[hide=MinecraftFox]I can’t say anything new about that one play.

As for your general play, this might also sound like a dead horse beating, but be active. If you aren’t active, there’s nothing else to comment on. If you can’t be active because of IRL stuff, that’s unfortunate and there’s nothing anyone can really do about that. Would recommend declaring V/LA, though.[/hide]
[hide=mathical8]It’s never fun being a miss. It’s never fun being the only miss. Glad you still enjoyed the game, though.

Your inactivity happened for reasons out of your control, and then you got tunneled for reasons out of your control. Town was wrong for the right reasons, though; they zeroed in on candy  immediately after your death.

I thought you did a good job of sounding really pure D2. At that point, Hannah was piling on accusations faster than you knew how to deal with, so succumbing to the pressure there is fair. Only advice would be generic stuff like be active, interact, communicate, etc. Eventually you’ll find a way to be towny if you keep doing that, and others will find ways to read you.[/hide]
